This family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Cacalia grisea belonging to the subfamily Asteroideae. Within the subfamily, Cacalia grisea is part of the tribe Senecioneae, which includes other daisy-like plants with similar characteristics. intro: Cacalia grisea, also known as grey cocklebur, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, Cacalia grisea has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cacalia grisea include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cacalia grisea is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as ointments and salves.
